item.title: Bitcoin Slides With Risk Assets as Trump’s Iran Ultimatum Looms
item.description: Bitcoin slipped in Asia Tuesday, as cryptocurrencies were caught up in the broader market volatility ahead of President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran.

item.title: Singapore Says US Removed ‘Inaccurate’ Trade Figure Amid Probes
item.description: Singapore said the US has withdrawn an “inaccurate statement” on bilateral trade balances that had underpinned Washington’s decision to launch probes into more than a dozen economies last month.

item.title: RBI Set to Hold Rates While Confronting Weak Rupee: Policy Guide
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India is likely to hold interest rates on Wednesday in its first policy decision since the Iran war, as it grapples with a sharply weaker rupee while trying to support economic growth.

item.title: Oil Shock Pushes Thailand’s Inflation Toward Positive Territory
item.description: Thailand’s yearlong stretch of falling prices is nearing an end, as higher oil costs and Middle East supply disruptions begin feeding through to inflation.

item.title: China’s Electric-Arc Steelmakers Regain Edge as Profits Recover
item.description: China’s electric-arc furnace steelmakers boosted weekly capacity utilization to the highest in more than two years, as the greener production method becomes more competitive.

item.title: Trumpworld’s Growing Sway in Arms Sales on Display in Rome
item.description: A summit of US, NATO and African brass featured crypto, AI and other businesses favored by the administration alongside traditional weapons vendors.

item.title: Surging Oil Prices to Keep Romania’s Interest Rates Unchanged
item.description: Romania is set to leave European Union’s highest interest rates steady as the surge in oil prices will likely fuel inflation that’s already running at close to double digits.

item.title: Japan 30-Year Bond Sale Sees Tepid Demand Ahead of Iran Deadline
item.description: Japan’s auction of 30-year notes saw muted demand as investors stayed cautious amid uncertainty about Middle East tensions.

item.title: Iran Amplifies LNG Shortage by Blocking Passages Through Hormuz
item.description: Iran has yet to allow a single carrier loaded with liquefied natural gas to pass through the Strait of Hormuz in weeks of war, according to traders involved in the transit, a ban that risks exacerbating global shortages.

item.title: Goldman Warns on Copper as Iran War Threatens Global Economy
item.description: Copper is vulnerable to further declines if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. warned, as metals markets brace for President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran to agree a deal or face sweeping attacks on civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Taiwan Pivots to Coal Power as War Disrupts Global LNG Market
item.description: Taiwan will increase coal-fired power generation to bolster energy security, as the war in the Middle East crimps gas supplies.

item.title: India’s Stock Rout Creates Openings for Bargain Hunters
item.description: Weak monsoon a bigger risk for the market than war, say Kotak strategists.

item.title: Australia’s Household Spending Climbs Ahead of Middle East War
item.description: Australia’s household spending rose in February, pointing to resilient demand ahead of the US-Israeli attack on Iran that triggered a surge in oil prices and renewed concerns about cost-of-living pressures.

item.title: China’s Coal Giants Bet on Chemicals as War Curbs Oil Supplies
item.description: Chinese coal companies are turning to chemicals manufacturing for growth, as war in the Persian Gulf constrains supply of the liquid fossil fuels more commonly used by the industry.

item.title: Oil Shock Sends Philippine Inflation Surging to 20-Month High
item.description: The Philippines’ inflation spiked in March to the highest in nearly two years as the Iran war choked energy supply and pushed up fuel prices.

item.title: Japan’s Households Cut Spending Even After Real Wages Advance
item.description: Japan’s households reduced spending for a third straight month even after real wages turned positive, underscoring the fragile state of domestic demand.

item.title: RBNZ Set to Extend Rate Pause, Keep Gauging Fuel Shock Fallout
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank is expected to keep interest rates unchanged on Wednesday and restate its willingness to look through the initial inflationary impact of surging fuel prices that threaten a stuttering recovery.

item.title: Yuan to Dodge Seasonal Slump on Iran War Resilience, Economy
item.description: China’s yuan is poised to defy its typical second-quarter slump as the nation’s resilience to the Iran war and a burgeoning economic recovery offset seasonal pressures.

item.title: Samsung Profit Up Eight-Fold After AI Chip Sales Defy War Fears
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. earned a stronger-than-expected eight-fold leap in quarterly profit, underscoring robust demand for memory chips used for artificial intelligence and data centers even amid uncertainties triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Holds Gains With US-Iran Deadline in Focus: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity futures were steady and oil held onto gains as President Donald Trump signaled a potential escalation of strikes on Iran as soon as Tuesday, overshadowing hopes for a ceasefire.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Hit Civilian Targets If Iran Doesn’t Open Hormuz
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast: Unpacking President Trump’s mixed rhetoric on Iran, the status of ceasefire talks and why he wants to collect tolls for the strait.

item.title: OpenAI Calls for Investigation Into Musk by California, Delaware
item.description: OpenAI urged the attorneys general of California and Delaware to investigate potential “improper and anti-competitive behavior” by Elon Musk in his efforts to block OpenAI from restructuring as a for-profit company.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Trump’s Deadline to Strike Iran
item.description: Gold steadied after a two-day decline as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s latest threat to destroy Iranian infrastructure if an agreement isn’t reached before a Tuesday deadline.

item.title: Oil Holds Near Highest Since 2022 as Trump Threats Escalate
item.description: US oil held near its highest close since June 2022 as President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

item.title: One-Dyas Expands Gas Output in Protected North Sea Marine Zone
item.description: One-Dyas BV has started production from a second well at its platform in the North Sea, lifting annual output at the site to about 1 billion cubic meters of gas.

item.title: Two Sigma Profits From Chaotic March, Beating Multistrat Peers
item.description: Two Sigma Investments’ biggest hedge funds generated standout returns during a frantic month for markets, edging out peers even as the firm grapples with executive infighting.

item.title: Anthropic Tops $30 Billion Run Rate, Seals Deal With Broadcom
item.description: Anthropic PBC said its revenue run rate has now topped $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025, and confirmed plans to work with Broadcom Inc. and Google to power its burgeoning operations.

item.title: Brazil Expands Fuel Tax Cuts, Subsidies as Iran War Carries On
item.description: Brazil’s government will expand federal tax cuts and subsidies on fuels as it attempts to shield consumers from rising prices due to the war in Iran, broadening measures it had previously unveiled amid the conflict.

item.title: Phillips 66 Sees Nearly $1 Billion in Losses as Oil Prices Surge
item.description: Phillips 66 estimated nearly $1 billion in losses from its short position in oil and other related commodity derivative contracts in the first quarter as the war in Iran sent crude and fuel prices skyrocketing.

item.title: Goldman Says It’s Ready to Pounce as Retail Flees Private Credit
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says a reliance on stickier, more patient institutional investors, rather than wealthy individuals, helped its $15.7 billion private credit fund narrowly escape the broader exodus plaguing peers this year.
